title: Scroll-Driven, Subgrid, Scramble, Tilt & Drawer
description: Five UI patterns: CSS scroll-driven animations, subgrid-aligned cards, text scramble decoder, 3D tilt+glare, and a spring-snap bottom drawer.

drops.title: Scroll-Driven Reveal Animations
drops.description: 8 CSS keyframe animations linked to scroll via animation-timeline: view() — zero JavaScript, all CSS.
drops.concept: The CSS Scroll-Driven Animations spec (Chrome 115+, Firefox 110+) lets you bind animation progress to the scroll position of a container or element's viewport entry. Using animation-timeline: view() and animation-range, each card triggers its own reveal animation independently the moment it enters the scrollport — no IntersectionObserver, no JS timers.
drops.source: Chrome Developers Scroll-Driven Animations guide + MDN Web Docs animation-timeline

interaction.mouse: Scroll down to see each effect trigger

drops.title: CSS Subgrid Alignment
drops.description: Toggle between chaotic stacked cards and perfectly row-aligned product cards using grid-template-rows: subgrid.
drops.concept: CSS Subgrid lets child elements of a grid item align to the parent grid's track definitions. With grid-row: span 6 + grid-template-rows: subgrid on each card, the browser makes all cards share the same six row heights — determined by the tallest content across the entire row. Icons, names, descriptions, specs, prices, and buttons all line up perfectly despite varying content lengths.
drops.source: Rachel Andrew's gridbyexample.com + MDN CSS Subgrid documentation

drops.title: Text Scramble Decoder
drops.description: Text chars cycle through random noise before resolving to target — 5 charset modes, hover to retrigger.
drops.concept: Per-character animation runs via requestAnimationFrame. Each character gets a randomised start and end frame. Before its start frame, it shows the original character. Between start and end, it shows a random character from the active charset (changing ~28% of frames for the flickering feel). After end frame, it locks to the target character. Five charset modes — hacker symbols, geometric shapes, digits, braille, and mixed — give the effect very different personalities.
drops.source: CodePen community text-scramble pattern, popularised by Tobias Ahlin & CSS-Tricks

interaction.mouse: Hover card to retrigger decode; click to advance phrase

drops.title: 3D Perspective Tilt + Glare
drops.description: 6 themed cards tilt toward cursor — CSS perspective + JS lerp + radial-gradient glare following mouse.
drops.concept: On mousemove, the cursor offset from the card centre is normalised to −1→+1 on each axis and multiplied by maxTilt degrees. lerp() smooths the current angles toward the target each rAF frame, giving a spring-like lag. A radial-gradient inside a ::glare div has its centre CSS custom property updated to match the cursor position, creating a specular highlight that sweeps across the surface. On mouseleave, the CSS transition property is restored, which spring-eases the card back flat.
drops.source: vanilla-tilt.js by micku7zu — reimplemented from scratch in plain JS

interaction.mouse: Hover any card — tilt follows cursor; glare highlights surface
interaction.touch: Touch and drag card to tilt

drops.title: Spring-Snap Bottom Drawer
drops.description: Mobile bottom sheet with 3 snap points — velocity-aware drag releases snap to peek, half, or full with spring timing.
drops.concept: The drawer tracks pointer velocity during drag (delta Y / delta time, smoothed per frame). On pointer release, a velocity-biased target is computed (currentY + velocity × 8) and compared to three snap points (peek = 100px visible, half = 48vh, full = 100%). The nearest snap wins. The drawer animates to it using a cubic-bezier(0.22, 0.68, 0, 1.2) which has a slight overshoot, simulating spring physics without a physics engine. A backdrop scrim's opacity is driven by the open fraction.
drops.source: iOS bottom sheet / Framer Motion drag snap pattern — vanilla reimplementation

interaction.mouse: Drag handle or header up/down; click snap buttons; click scrim to close
interaction.touch: Drag from handle; tap snap buttons
